summary: Scarlet Begonias solo shines, solid set with Big Railroad Blues
blurb: The second‑set sequence of Scarlet Begonias → Fire on the Mountain flows into a heartfelt Black Peter, while the first set packs energy with Big Railroad Blues and a rousing opening. Jerry’s guitar work is strong throughout, though some listeners note the show feels a bit generic overall.
review:
December 28, 1986 at the Henry J. Kaiser Convention Center delivered a solid, if not spectacular, Grateful Dead outing. The first set kicked off with a chilly “Cold Rain and Snow” and built momentum through “New Minglewood Blues,” “Row Jimmy,” and a punchy “Big Railroad Blues.” The second set provided the highlight moments: a soaring solo on “Scarlet Begonias,” the seamless segue into “Fire on the Mountain,” and an emotionally resonant “Black Peter” that showcased Jerry’s heartfelt phrasing. While the performance is generally steady, several reviewers felt the show was short‑handed and a touch generic, with no major surprises beyond the noted high points.

Recording-wise, the matrix capture (gd86-12-28.sbd-matrix.miller.11495.sbeok.shnf) stands out for its excellent instrument separation and crisp sound, making it the recommended listen despite occasional low‑end roughness on “Cold Rain.” The soundboard version suffers from muddy mix and drop‑outs, and audience tapes are uneven, so the matrix source best conveys the band’s dynamics. Overall, this show is a decent entry for fans looking for a reliable set with a standout Scarlet Begonias solo and solid Dead energy.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Mixed sentiment across five recordings, with two clearly positive reviews and three mixed. Positive language about Scarlet Begonias solo, Black Peter, and Big Railroad Blues yields a modest boost; no consistent negative criticism to offset it.

best_recording.reason: Excellent matrix mix with clear instrument separation; highlights Scarlet Begonias solo, Big Railroad Blues, and Black Peter while maintaining overall crisp sound.

key_highlights: Soaring Scarlet Begonias solo that lifts the second set.
High‑energy “Big Railroad Blues” drives the first set forward.
Heartfelt rendition of “Black Peter” showcases Jerry’s emotive playing.
"Cold Rain and Snow" opener captured on a nostalgic audience tape.

band_performance.Jerry: Back in great form with a heartfelt Black Peter and strong guitar tone on Scarlet Begonias; emotionally compelling though at times a bit reserved, still delivers solid solo work.
band_performance.Bob: Noted for eye‑contact with Jerry and an amazed reaction to the second solo on Scarlet Begonias, adding visual excitement to the performance.
